text.title: Shutdown Customer Email Pack Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Shutdown Customer Email Pack application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: First announce email MUST be sent at least 30 days before service-off; shorter windows rejected unless legal force majeure applies.
rule.rationale: 30-day minimum gives customers time to export data, find replacements, and dispute charges without escalating to chargebacks.

rule.statement: Second email MUST contain a concrete refund OR migration offer — 'thanks for support' without remedy is rejected.
rule.rationale: Refunds (or a credible migration) are the difference between graceful and 'took my money and disappeared'.

rule.statement: Sequence MUST be exactly three emails — announce, refund/migrate, final thanks — at T-30, T-14, T+1.
rule.rationale: Single-email shutdowns lose 30%+ refund-eligible customers to inattention; cadence ensures the deadline is unmissable.

rule.statement: Every email MUST be sent from a named human (founder name + photo + signature); noreply@ and generic 'team' aliases are rejected.
rule.rationale: At shutdown the customer needs to know who is accountable; bare alias signals abandonment.

rule.statement: Copy MUST state the real reason in plain language (≥1 honest sentence); corporate-speak phrases ('exciting new chapter', 'evolving') are rejected.
rule.rationale: Customers can read corporate-speak and resent being patronised at the moment of loss; honest cause framing earns the most respect.
